steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks which conclusion is supported by the data. The passage establishes that queen honeybees exceeded the positive-charge threshold needed to attract stamens. The graph shows charge levels for multiple bee species relative to that threshold.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "red mason bees and paper wasps, with greater maximum charges than queen honeybees have, cannot attract the stamens.": contradicts the data, which shows that meeting the threshold produces attraction. ✗
  • "paper wasps tend to repel the stamens.": no basis for repulsion is shown in the graph or passage. ✗
  • "red mason bees can also attract the stamens.": the graph shows red mason bee charge meets or exceeds the same threshold as queen honeybees, so stamen attraction follows. ✓
  • "the threshold positive charge for a pollinator to attract the stamens must be greater than 200 pC.": the graph does not indicate the exact numerical threshold. ✗
That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
